summary:
Claim: The upper first molar has three major fossae.  
Evidence: - Standard dental anatomy texts (e.g., Hargreaves, Ricketts) describe one central fossa and two lateral fossae. - No credible evidence supports a deviation from this norm in healthy adult maxillary first molars.  
Answer: A

Answer: A. Answer choice: A
